flashcards.front: What is a Doubly Linked List?
flashcards.back: A linked list where each node has two pointers: next (to next node) and prev (to previous node). Allows bidirectional traversal.

O(1) in doubly linked list
O(n) in singly linked list

Reason: Direct access to prev pointer eliminates need to traverse.

flashcards.front: Memory Overhead of Doubly Linked List
flashcards.back:
~50% more memory per node compared to singly (extra prev pointer).

Trade-off: More memory for better operation efficiency.

1. Browser history (back/forward)
2. LRU Cache implementation
3. Text editor undo/redo
4. Music player (prev/next)
